etymology.description: 声(聲) (shēng, 'stone chime') + 殳(殳) (shū, 'hand holding a hammer or a mallet') = a stone chime being struck by a mallet -> sound of metal or stone
etymology.interpretation: The character 殸(qīng, 'sound of metal or stone') represents the concept of producing sound by striking a chime-like stone with a mallet, conveying the idea of resonant sounds akin to those produced by metal or stone instruments.

parts.english: hand holding a hammer or a mallet
